verses.text: Benjamites — Saul's own kinsmen — come to David at Ziklag while he is still hunted, skilled archers ambidextrous in war. They join the rejected king in his day of danger. True faith commits to God's anointed before his glory comes.

More mighty men gather to David at Ziklag, leaving their own to follow him. They cast their lot with the fugitive, not the throne. Those who own God's king in his rejection share his honour in his reign.

More mighty men gather to David at Ziklag, leaving their own to follow him. They cast their lot with the fugitive, not the throne. Those who own God's king in his rejection share his honour in his reign.

The Gadites separate themselves to David in the wilderness, men of war 'whose faces were like the faces of lions, and as swift as the roes.' Bold and ready, they sought the king in hardship. Such are the servants God gathers to His cause.

The Gadites separate themselves to David in the wilderness, men of war 'whose faces were like the faces of lions, and as swift as the roes.' Bold and ready, they sought the king in hardship. Such are the servants God gathers to His cause.

The Gadites separate themselves to David in the wilderness, men of war 'whose faces were like the faces of lions, and as swift as the roes.' Bold and ready, they sought the king in hardship. Such are the servants God gathers to His cause.

These Gadites crossed the flooded Jordan and routed their foes on both banks. They would not let a swollen river stop them. Faith presses through every obstacle to reach the king and fight his battles.

When Benjamin and Judah come, David tests them, and the Spirit moves Amasai to pledge, 'Thine are we, David... for thy God helpeth thee.' Here is wholehearted allegiance. We give ourselves to God's king because God is with him.

Men of Manasseh defect to David, and day by day more come, until there is 'a great host, like the host of God.' The band of outcasts swells into an army. God builds His king's strength soul by soul, day by day.

The tribal companies that came to Hebron to turn the kingdom to David are numbered, 'according to the word of the LORD.' All Israel rallies to God's choice. What He decreed by Samuel now comes to pass through a united people.

The roll includes priests, Levites, and the men of Issachar, 'who had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do.' Discernment is prized among the warriors. God's people need not only strength but wisdom to read their day.

Tribe after tribe sends its thousands, including Zebulun, 'not of double heart.' Whole-hearted, undivided loyalty marks them. God is served best by those who come to Him with no divided allegiance.

All these come 'with a perfect heart' to make David king, and Israel is 'of one heart,' feasting three days with great joy. Unity and gladness crown the coronation. When God's people rally whole-heartedly to His king, there is joy in the land.
